sample_records.inspection_comment:
OWNERSHIP CHANGE INFORMATION 

NEW OWNER: DYNASTY INTERNAIONAL TRADING INC.
NEW FACILITY NAME: DYNASTY BBQ

The applicant has completed the change of ownership application process for an Environmental Health Permit. 

The permit category for this facility is FP14. An invoice for the permit fee in the amount of  $1723.00 will be mailed to the billing address on the application. Payment must be submitted within 10 days of receipt of the invoice. The owner is responsible for contacting our department if an invoice is not received and remit payment within 30 days. 

The Environmental Health Permit will be effective: 08/01/2024 - 07/31/2025. This report serves as a temporary permit. However, the permit will be deemed invalid if the permit fee is not paid in full within 30 days from the date of this report. Okay to Operate.

Post permit in public view. 

Structural Review inspection conducted on 07/15/2024.
 
Obtain food safety manager certificate within 60 days. All other food employees must have valid food handler cards within 30 days from hire date.  Food Safety Manager Certificate and Food Handler cards shall be available for review.

Information on a Food Safety Certificate and Food Handler cards are available at https://www.sccgov.org/sites/cpd/programs/fsp/Pages/FSC.aspx. 

Facility shall notify district specialist should there be a change of number of employees or change in menu. District specialist shall be notified prior to the addition, replacement, or removal of any equipment. 
Any installation of new equipment, without prior notification to district specialist/Plan Check is subject to impound. Unapproved equipment will not be allowed for use.

Facility is recommended to have professional pest control services. 

Facility shall have a procedure in place for cleaning meat slicer after every 4 hours during use.

sample_records.description: Proper reheating procedures for hot holding
sample_records.violation_comment: Measured black beans in steam table between 50F steam table was used to reheat beans. [CA] Reheat beans on stove to 165F and hot hold at 135F and above. [COS] Beans were reheated to 165F and hot held at 135F and above.

sample_records.description: Proper reheating procedures for hot holding
sample_records.violation_comment: Measured the following PHFs holding between 110F - 116F in warmer unit at front service line (left side): brisket and nacho cheese. Per operator, PHFs was previously reheated on cooks line for less than 1 hours. [CA] Reheating for hot holding shall be done rapidly, and the time the food is between 41°F and 165°F shall not exceed two hours. [COS] Operator directed to reheat PHFs to 165F.
